100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On April 20, 1926, the United States celebrated the first-ever National Park Week, emphasizing the importance of preserving natural resources. Meanwhile, tensions continued to rise in the Middle East as British forces clashed with Arab nationalists in Palestine. In Europe, Germany faced economic challenges as negotiations for reparations payments stalled, raising concerns about stability in the region.
